description_long: This is a dark-colored, single-propeller military-style airplane with a robust fuselage and a distinctive gull-wing design. The wings are mounted low on the fuselage and are equipped with multiple hardpoints for external stores, such as bombs or rockets. The cockpit is positioned forward and is enclosed with a clear canopy, providing good visibility for the pilot. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, with a tailwheel landing gear configuration. The surface of the airplane appears metallic with some weathering, and the overall design suggests a World War II-era fighter aircraft, possibly inspired by the Vought F4U Corsair. The airplane is detailed with visible rivets, panel lines, and underwing armaments, giving it a realistic and functional appearance.
description: A dark, single-propeller military-style airplane with gull wings, forward enclosed cockpit, and visible underwing armaments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the propeller, forward cockpit canopy, and the distinctive gull-wing shape.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the low-mounted gull wings, external stores, and tailwheel landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section, vertical stabilizer, and the full wingspan.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the engine cowling, wing hardpoints, and tailwheel.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, inverted gull-wing configuration with straight leading and trailing edges, no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a three-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed gear and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy integrated into the fuselage.
